name: Mzansi Mix Vol. 3
description: South African nature, food, travel, music, and tech — a Mzansi knowledge mix!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: How many strings does a standard guitar have?
questions.explanation: A standard guitar has 6 strings. Whether you're playing maskandi or rock, it's always 6!

questions.question: What is biltong made from?
questions.explanation: Biltong is dried, cured meat — usually beef or game. It's South Africa's favourite snack and we take it very seriously!

questions.question: Which is bigger: Table Mountain or a table?
questions.explanation: Table Mountain is over 1,000 metres high. Even the biggest dining table can't compete with that! Don't overthink this one.

questions.question: What colour is rooibos tea?
questions.explanation: Rooibos means 'red bush' in Afrikaans, and the tea brews a beautiful reddish-brown colour. Proudly South African since forever!

questions.question: If you drive from Johannesburg heading south, which ocean would you eventually reach?
questions.explanation: Heading south from Joburg takes you towards the Indian Ocean coast (think Durban way). Lekker beach vibes!

questions.question: Which South African city is known as the 'Mother City'?
questions.explanation: Cape Town is called the Mother City because it was the first European settlement in South Africa, founded in 1652. She's the OG!

questions.question: What is a melktert?
questions.explanation: Melktert literally means 'milk tart' in Afrikaans. It's a creamy, cinnamon-dusted custard tart that's pure comfort food. Every tannie has her own recipe!

questions.question: What genre of music is DJ Maphorisa most associated with?
questions.explanation: DJ Maphorisa is one of the godfathers of amapiano, the genre that took Mzansi and the world by storm. Yanos for life!

questions.question: Which animal is on the South African coat of arms?
questions.explanation: The secretary bird appears on South Africa's coat of arms, symbolising protection and growth. It's also a serious snake-stomper!

questions.question: What is a 'braai' in South African culture?
questions.explanation: A braai is a barbecue — but don't ever call it that to a South African! It's a way of life, not just cooking. National Braai Day is 24 September, lekker!

questions.question: Which province is the Kruger National Park mainly located in?
questions.explanation: Kruger stretches across both Limpopo and Mpumalanga, but the majority of the park and its main camps are in Mpumalanga. Pack your binoculars!

questions.question: What is a koeksister?
options: A braided fried dough soaked in syrup
questions.explanation: A koeksister is a braided, deep-fried dough treat soaked in sweet syrup until it's crispy on the outside and syrupy inside. Sticky fingers guaranteed!

questions.question: Which South African tech company created the messaging app Mxit?
questions.explanation: Mxit was built by MXit Lifestyle (Pty) Ltd in Stellenbosch. At its peak it had over 50 million users across Africa. SA was ahead of the game before WhatsApp took over!

questions.question: What is the fynbos biome unique to?
options: The Western Cape of South Africa
questions.explanation: Fynbos is a unique shrubland vegetation found almost exclusively in the Western Cape. It's one of only six floral kingdoms on Earth — and the smallest! Proudly ours.

questions.question: Put these animals in order from SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) African elephant, (2) Meerkat, (3) Springbok, (4) Hippopotamus
questions.explanation: Meerkat (~730g), Springbok (~40kg), Hippo (~1,500kg), African Elephant (~6,000kg). From tiny to mighty in the African bush!

questions.question: Which famous South African inventor created the Kreepy Krauly automatic pool cleaner?
questions.explanation: Ferdinand Chauvier, a hydraulic engineer from Johannesburg, invented the Kreepy Krauly in 1974. It's now used in pools worldwide. SA innovation at its finest!

questions.question: What is bunny chow?
options: A hollowed-out bread loaf filled with curry
questions.explanation: Bunny chow is a Durban classic — a hollowed-out quarter, half, or full loaf of white bread filled with curry. No bunnies involved! It's named after the Bania merchants who popularised it.

questions.question: Which South African city hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
questions.explanation: The 2010 World Cup final was held at Soccer City (now FNB Stadium) in Johannesburg. Spain beat the Netherlands 1-0 with that iconic Iniesta goal. Ke nako!

questions.question: What is the traditional Zulu shield called?
questions.explanation: The isihlangu is the large Zulu war shield made from cowhide. The assegai is the spear, the knobkierie is a club, and umkhonto means spear too. Warriors knew their gear!

questions.question: Which South African national park is famous for its elephant population and is near Port Elizabeth?
questions.explanation: Addo Elephant National Park near Port Elizabeth is home to over 600 elephants. It's the only park in the world with the Big 7 — the Big 5 plus the great white shark and southern right whale!

questions.question: What plant is used to make rooibos tea?
questions.explanation: Rooibos comes from Aspalathus linearis, a plant that grows only in the Cederberg region of South Africa. Camellia sinensis makes regular tea, Cyclopia makes honeybush. Lekker local!

questions.question: Which South African musician is known as the 'Queen of African Pop'?
questions.explanation: Yvonne Chaka Chaka is known as the 'Princess of Africa' and 'Queen of African Pop'. Miriam Makeba was 'Mama Africa' and Brenda Fassie was 'MaBrrr'. SA has queens for days!

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in potjiekos?
questions.explanation: Potjiekos is a slow-cooked stew made with layers of meat and vegetables in a cast-iron pot over coals. The secret? Don't stir it! Let the layers do their magic.

questions.question: Which company did South African-born Elon Musk co-found before Tesla?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk, born in Pretoria, co-founded X.com which merged with Confinity to become PayPal. He was part of the 'PayPal Mafia' before going on to Tesla and SpaceX. Mzansi-born billionaire!

questions.question: Put these distances in order from SHORTEST to LONGEST: (1) Joburg to Pretoria (~58km), (2) Cape Town to Stellenbosch (~50km), (3) Durban to Pietermaritzburg (~80km), (4) Bloemfontein to Maseru (~140km)
questions.explanation: Cape Town-Stellenbosch (~50km), Joburg-Pretoria (~58km), Durban-PMB (~80km), Bloem-Maseru (~140km). Know your SA distances, road tripper!

questions.question: Which pass connects the Garden Route town of Knysna to the inland town of Oudtshoorn?
questions.explanation: Prince Alfred's Pass is a gravel road connecting Knysna to Avontuur/Oudtshoorn area through stunning indigenous forest. The Outeniqua Pass connects George to Oudtshoorn. Both are lekker drives!

questions.question: What is 'pap' (also called mieliepap) made from?
questions.explanation: Pap is made from maize meal (mielie meal) and is a staple food across South Africa. Stiff pap, soft pap, krummelpap — there's a pap for every occasion!

questions.question: Which South African app became Africa's first 'super app' for payments, messaging, and lifestyle services?
questions.explanation: Vodapay, launched by Vodacom, positioned itself as Africa's first super app — combining payments, shopping, and services in one place. SA fintech is going places!

questions.question: Which South African bird can run up to 70 km/h, making it the fastest bird on land?
questions.explanation: The ostrich can sprint at up to 70 km/h, making it the fastest bird on land (and the fastest two-legged animal!). They can't fly, but they don't need to with those legs!

questions.question: What does the Afrikaans word 'lekker' mean?
questions.explanation: Lekker means nice, great, cool, delicious — basically anything good! It's the most versatile word in SA slang. 'How's the food?' 'Lekker, bru!'

questions.question: Which wine region in South Africa is the oldest and most famous?
questions.explanation: Constantia is SA's oldest wine-producing region, dating back to 1685. Its sweet wine was even a favourite of Napoleon! Stellenbosch is more famous today, but Constantia was first.

questions.question: What is the name of the traditional Xhosa ceremony where young men go through initiation into manhood?
questions.explanation: Ulwaluko is the Xhosa male initiation ceremony, a sacred tradition marking the transition from boyhood to manhood. It's deeply respected in Xhosa culture.

questions.question: Which South African tech entrepreneur became the first African civilian in space in 2002?
questions.explanation: Mark Shuttleworth from Cape Town paid $20 million to fly to the International Space Station aboard a Russian Soyuz rocket. He later founded Ubuntu Linux. Legend!

questions.question: What type of shark is most commonly spotted during cage diving in Gansbaai, South Africa?
questions.explanation: Gansbaai is known as the Great White Shark capital of the world! Shark Alley between Dyer Island and Geyser Rock is one of the best spots to see these apex predators up close.

questions.question: Put these SA music genres in order from OLDEST origin to NEWEST: (1) Amapiano, (2) Kwaito, (3) Mbaqanga, (4) Gqom
questions.explanation: Mbaqanga (1960s), Kwaito (1990s), Gqom (2010s), Amapiano (late 2010s). SA music keeps evolving, and we love it!

questions.question: Which mountain pass in KwaZulu-Natal reaches over 2,800m and requires a 4x4 to drive?
questions.explanation: Sani Pass connects KZN to Lesotho and climbs to about 2,876m. It's one of the most dramatic 4x4 routes in Africa. The reward? A beer at the highest pub in Africa at the top!

questions.question: What is the king protea's claim to fame in South Africa?
options: It's the tallest flower in Africa
It only blooms once every 10 years
It's the oldest flowering plant on Earth
questions.explanation: The king protea (Protea cynaroides) is South Africa's national flower. It's part of the fynbos biome and appears on the SA cricket team's logo. A true Mzansi beauty!

questions.question: Which South African artist's hit 'Jerusalema' went viral worldwide during the COVID-19 pandemic?
questions.explanation: Master KG's 'Jerusalema' featuring Nomcebo Zikode became a global sensation in 2020, with dance challenges from Angola to Switzerland. SA music conquering the world!

questions.question: What is the Drakensberg also known as in Zulu?
questions.explanation: The Drakensberg is called uKhahlamba in Zulu, meaning 'barrier of spears'. The Afrikaans name means 'dragon mountains'. Both names capture how dramatic these peaks are!

questions.question: Which South African mobile payment system was a pioneer in QR code payments when it launched in 2013?
questions.explanation: SnapScan, developed in Stellenbosch and acquired by Standard Bank, was one of the first QR code-based payment apps in the world. SA fintech was way ahead of the curve!

questions.question: Which South African Nobel Prize winner discovered the CT scan?
questions.explanation: Allan Cormack from Johannesburg co-invented the CT scan and won the 1979 Nobel Prize in Medicine. He shared it with Godfrey Hounsfield. SA brains changing the world!

questions.question: What is the 'Cape Floral Kingdom' and why is it remarkable?
options: The largest flower market in Africa
The smallest of Earth's six floral kingdoms, with extraordinary biodiversity
A botanical garden in Cape Town
questions.explanation: The Cape Floral Kingdom is the smallest of Earth's six floral kingdoms but has the highest density of plant species. Over 9,000 species, 70% found nowhere else on Earth. UNESCO World Heritage, no less!

questions.question: What is a 'gatsby' in Cape Town food culture?
options: A long roll stuffed with chips and various fillings
questions.explanation: A gatsby is a massive sandwich — a long roll packed with hot chips (fries), plus your choice of polony, steak, masala steak, or other fillings. One gatsby can feed a whole family. Cape Flats legend!

questions.question: A sundial in a Stellenbosch garden shows 2pm. If you look at it in a mirror, what time does it appear to show?
questions.explanation: A mirror reverses the image. On a clock face, 2 o'clock reflected becomes 10 o'clock. The mirror flips left-right, so the hands appear at the mirror position. Clever, ne?

questions.question: Put these SA creatures in order of MOST to FEWEST legs: (1) Shongololo (millipede), (2) Crab, (3) Spider, (4) Beetle
questions.explanation: Shongololo (hundreds of legs!), Crab (10 legs), Spider (8 legs), Beetle (6 legs). From creepy-crawly champion to six-legged beetle. Know your critters!

questions.question: Which ancient hominin fossil, discovered near Johannesburg, was nicknamed 'Mrs Ples'?
questions.explanation: Mrs Ples is a famous Australopithecus africanus skull found at Sterkfontein Caves in the Cradle of Humankind, dated to about 2.1 million years ago. SA is literally where humanity began!

questions.question: What is maskandi music, and which instrument is central to it?
options: Zulu folk music featuring the guitar or concertina
questions.explanation: Maskandi is a traditional Zulu folk music genre where the guitar (acoustic, often with unique tunings) or concertina takes centre stage. Artists like Shwi noMtekhala and Phuzekhemisi are legends!

questions.question: You're lost in the Cederberg mountains with no compass. At noon, the sun is roughly north (Southern Hemisphere). Your shadow points south. If you face your shadow, which direction is to your LEFT?
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere at noon, the sun is roughly north. Facing your shadow (south), east is to your left. This is the opposite of the Northern Hemisphere! Knowing this could save your life in the veld.

questions.question: Which rare antelope, once thought extinct, was rediscovered in South Africa and is now bred at a nature reserve in Limpopo?
questions.explanation: The bontebok was down to just 17 individuals in the 1830s, making it critically close to extinction. Conservation efforts brought them back, and Bontebok National Park near Swellendam was created just for them!

questions.question: What is the significance of the MeerKAT radio telescope in the Northern Cape?
options: It studies meerkats with radio tracking
It's a precursor to the Square Kilometre Array (SKA), the world's largest radio telescope
It provides internet to rural communities
questions.explanation: MeerKAT is a 64-dish radio telescope near Carnarvon in the Northern Cape. It's a precursor to the SKA, which will be the most sensitive radio telescope ever built. SA is at the frontier of space science!

questions.question: Which South African fossil site, discovered by Lee Berger in 2013, yielded a new species of human ancestor?
questions.explanation: Rising Star Cave in the Cradle of Humankind yielded Homo naledi, discovered by Lee Berger's team in 2013. Over 1,500 fossil elements were found — the largest collection of a single hominin species in Africa. Mind-blowing!

questions.question: What is 'umqombothi' and what is it made from?
options: A fermented milk drink made from cow's milk
A traditional beer made from maize, sorghum malt, and water
A fruit wine made from marula berries
A honey-based mead from the Eastern Cape
questions.explanation: Umqombothi is a traditional African beer brewed from maize (corn), maize malt, sorghum malt, yeast, and water. It has a thick, gritty texture and is shared from a communal pot at celebrations. Yvonne Chaka Chaka even sang about it!

questions.question: What makes the quiver tree (Aloidendron dichotomum) culturally significant to the San people of the Northern Cape?
options: They used the bark to make poison
They hollowed out the branches to make quivers for arrows
They worshipped it as a sacred tree
They brewed tea from its leaves
questions.explanation: The San (Bushmen) hollowed out the soft, fibrous branches of the quiver tree to make quivers for their arrows — that's how the tree got its name! The Quiver Tree Forest near Keetmanshoop is a national monument.

questions.question: Which South African DJ won the Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Music Album in 2022?
questions.explanation: Black Coffee (Nkosinathi Innocent Maphumulo) won the 2022 Grammy for his album 'Subconsciously'. He's from Durban and is one of the biggest DJs on the planet. Mzansi to the world!

questions.question: Rank these SA national parks by size from SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) Kruger National Park, (2) Table Mountain National Park, (3) Kgalagadi Transfrontier Park, (4) Addo Elephant National Park
questions.explanation: Table Mountain NP (~221 km²), Addo Elephant NP (~1,640 km²), Kruger NP (~19,485 km²), Kgalagadi Transfrontier (~38,000 km² total). The Kgalagadi is massive — it spans SA and Botswana! Only a true nature lover gets this right.
